varieties.name: Virginia Jumbo
varieties.traits: Virginia bunch/semi-spreading, open-pollinated; Very large pods (1-2 nuts per pod), 140-150 day maturity, spreading growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high (2,000-3,000 kg/ha under good conditions)
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, seed companies

varieties.name: NC-V11
varieties.origin: North Carolina State University, USA (modern hybrid)
varieties.traits: Virginia bunch, hybrid selection; Large pods, upright bunch habit, 135-145 day maturity
varieties.availability: Commercial seed suppliers, Peanut Company of Australia (PCA)

varieties.name: Florunner
varieties.traits: Runner, heirloom (50+ years); Medium pods, runner growth habit, 130-140 day maturity, dominated world markets 1970-1990
varieties.yield_notes: Very high for its era (2,500-3,500 kg/ha), 20-25% higher than competitors
varieties.availability: Limited - mostly heritage seed savers, some specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Georgia Green
varieties.traits: Runner, modern hybrid with disease resistance; Medium pods, runner habit, 140 day maturity, tomato spotted wilt virus resistance

varieties.name: Southern Runner
varieties.traits: Runner, disease-resistant hybrid; Medium pods, runner habit, 135-145 days, first variety with leaf spot resistance

varieties.name: Spanish (Generic)
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, open-pollinated; Small pods (2-3 nuts), compact upright bunch, 90-110 day maturity, red-brown skins
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, farmers markets, Daley's Fruit Tree, seed companies

varieties.name: Spanish Red
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, open-pollinated; Small pods, distinctive red skin, bunch habit, 100-110 days

varieties.name: Tennessee Red Valencia
varieties.traits: Valencia bunch, open-pollinated heirloom; 3-6 small kernels per pod, red skins, upright bunch, 90-110 day maturity
varieties.availability: Specialty seed companies (international), heritage seed exchanges, some online suppliers

varieties.name: Valencia (Generic)
varieties.traits: Valencia bunch, open-pollinated; 3+ small kernels per pod, red-skinned, bunch habit, 90-110 days
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online seed suppliers

varieties.name: Carolina African Runner
varieties.origin: West Africa → South Carolina, USA (1690s, nearly extinct, revived 2013)
varieties.traits: Runner, heirloom landrace (300+ years), open-pollinated; Very small pods, runner habit, 110-130 days, brought to Americas by enslaved Africans
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than modern types (1,200-1,800 kg/ha) but flavor compensates

varieties.name: Dixie Runner
varieties.origin: University of Florida (released 1943, Fred Hull & William Carver)
varieties.traits: Runner, heirloom (80+ years), open-pollinated; Medium pods, runner habit, 135-145 days, first artificially hybridized peanut (1928)

varieties.name: Streeton
varieties.traits: Virginia bunch, Australian-bred hybrid; Large pods, upright bunch, 140-150 days, bred for dryland South Burnett conditions
varieties.availability: Peanut Company of Australia (PCA), Queensland agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Middleton
varieties.traits: Virginia bunch, high-oleic hybrid; Large pods, upright bunch, 145 days, high-oleic oil profile for extended shelf life
varieties.availability: PCA commercial seed program

varieties.name: Wheeler
varieties.traits: Runner, high-oleic hybrid; Medium pods, runner habit, 140 days, high-oleic, nut-in-shell trade
varieties.availability: PCA commercial seed program

varieties.name: Conder
varieties.traits: Runner, hybrid; Medium pods, runner habit, 135-145 days

varieties.name: TMV-2
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU), mass selection from 'Gudiya tham Bunch', released 1940
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, heirloom (85+ years), open-pollinated, INDIGENOUS to South India; Small pods, compact upright bunch, 105 days maturity, most widely grown variety in South India for decades

varieties.name: TMV-7
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, heirloom (55+ years), open-pollinated; Small pods, bunch habit, 105 days, consistent seed size and oil content
varieties.availability: Agricultural universities, certified seed dealers in Kerala/Tamil Nadu, local suppliers

varieties.name: TMV-9
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, open-pollinated hybrid; Small pods, upright bunch, 110 days
varieties.availability: Kerala/Tamil Nadu agricultural departments, certified seed sources

varieties.name: TMV-12
varieties.origin: TNAU, pureline selection from Ugandan culture, released 1978
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, open-pollinated selection; Small pods, compact bunch, 105 days
varieties.availability: Kerala Agricultural University stations, Tamil Nadu seed sources, local dealers

varieties.name: TMV-13
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, red kernel type; Small pods, bunch habit, distinctive red kernels

varieties.name: JL-24
varieties.origin: India, mass selection from Taiwan variety
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, hybrid selection; Small pods, compact bunch, 100 days, early maturity

varieties.name: GG-2
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, heirloom (40+ years), open-pollinated; Small pods, upright bunch, early maturity
varieties.availability: Gujarat seed sources (JAU), some Kerala agricultural suppliers, oil mills

varieties.name: ICGS-11
varieties.origin: ICRISAT (International Crops Research Institute for Semi-Arid Tropics)
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, international hybrid; Small pods, bunch habit, drought tolerance, wide adaptation
varieties.availability: ICRISAT seed program, Kerala Agricultural University, state agricultural departments

varieties.name: Kadiri-2
varieties.origin: ANGRAU Groundnut Research Centre, Kadiri, Andhra Pradesh
varieties.traits: Virginia bunch, semi-spreading, open-pollinated; Medium pods, semi-spreading habit, 120-130 days
varieties.availability: ANGRAU Kadiri research station, Andhra Pradesh seed sources, some Kerala dealers

varieties.name: Kadiri-3
varieties.traits: Virginia runner, semi-spreading, open-pollinated; Medium-large pods, semi-spreading to runner habit, 120-130 days
varieties.availability: ANGRAU sources, Andhra Pradesh/Karnataka seed dealers, premium market suppliers

varieties.name: Kadiri-6
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, hybrid; Small-medium pods, bunch habit, 110-120 days, most popular in Andhra Pradesh (80% adoption)

varieties.name: Kadiri-9
varieties.traits: Virginia bunch, modern hybrid; Medium pods, bunch habit, 115-125 days, pest and disease resistant, bold kernels
varieties.availability: ANGRAU certified seed, Andhra Pradesh sources, increasing in Kerala seed system

varieties.name: GG-20
varieties.traits: Semi-spreading, open-pollinated; Medium pods, semi-spreading habit, 120-130 days, dominant Gujarat variety
varieties.availability: JAU Junagadh, Gujarat seed sources, oil industry suppliers, limited Kerala availability

varieties.name: ICGV 91114
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, early maturing, drought-resistant hybrid; Small pods, compact bunch, 95-105 days, exceptional drought tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Good drought performance (1,600-2,200 kg/ha even in low rainfall)
varieties.availability: ICRISAT seed program, expanding in South India, Kerala Agricultural University trials, NGO seed distribution

varieties.name: ICGV 86325
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, disease-resistant hybrid; Small pods, bunch habit, 115-125 days, tolerant to rust, late leaf spot, bud necrosis disease
varieties.availability: ICRISAT, Kerala Agricultural University, state seed corporations, agricultural departments

varieties.name: Girnar-4 (ICGV 15083)
varieties.traits: Virginia runner, high-oleic hybrid; Medium-large pods, runner habit, 125-135 days, India's first high-oleic variety

varieties.name: Girnar-5 (ICGV 15090)
varieties.traits: Virginia runner, high-oleic hybrid; Medium-large pods, runner habit, 125-135 days, high-oleic oil profile

varieties.name: TMV-11
varieties.origin: TNAU, selection from 'East African Variety', released 1977
varieties.traits: Valencia bunch, open-pollinated; 3-4 kernels per pod, compact bunch, 100 days maturity

varieties.name: Bold (Runner Type)
varieties.origin: India - commercial classification for large-seeded runner types
varieties.traits: Runner/Virginia types, various genetics; Large pods and kernels, varied maturity, premium market classification
varieties.availability: Commercial traders, seed dealers specializing in export varieties, larger agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Java (Spanish Type)
varieties.origin: India - commercial classification for Spanish bunch types (white round shape)
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch types, various genetics; Small round pods, white/tan kernels, short maturity, hand-to-mouth consumption market

varieties.name: Red Natal
varieties.traits: Spanish type with red skins; Small pods, distinctive red/pink skin, specialty market
varieties.availability: Specialty traders, confectionery suppliers, some Kerala agricultural markets, traditional sweet makers

varieties.name: TAG-24
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, hybrid; Small-medium pods, bunch habit, 110-120 days
varieties.availability: Andhra Pradesh/Karnataka sources, ICRISAT program, expanding in Kerala

varieties.name: Narayani
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, hybrid; Small pods, bunch habit, early maturity

varieties.name: CO (GN) 4
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, hybrid; Small pods, bunch habit, disease resistant (rust & late leaf spot)

varieties.name: VRI (GN) 5
varieties.traits: Spanish bunch, hybrid; Small pods, bunch habit, suitable for kharif season
